quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 9 Quiz 10
quiz.subtitle: Electrical Energy, Power, Resistivity and Cost
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: What is the equivalence of 1 kWh in Joules?
1 kWh is equal to how many units of electricity?
questions.explanation: In electrical billing, 1 unit of electricity is exactly equal to 1 kWh of energy consumed.

questions.question: Which component is designed to interrupt an electrical circuit when the current exceeds a specific threshold, thereby protecting other devices from damage?
questions.explanation: A fuse consists of a metal strip or wire that melts when too much current flows through it. This breaks the electrical circuit, preventing damage to other components from excessive current.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a correct formula for electrical power?
questions.explanation: P = V/I is incorrect — V/I gives resistance (R), not power. The correct formulas are P = VI, P = I²R, and P = V²/R.

questions.question: A copper wire of resistance 10 Ω has a length of 2 m and cross-sectional area 4 × 10⁻⁶ m². What is the resistivity of the wire?
A wire has resistivity 1.72 × 10⁻⁸ Ω·m, cross-sectional area 1 × 10⁻⁶ m², and resistance 0.344 Ω. What is the length of the wire?
If the length and cross-sectional area of a conductor are both doubled, what happens to the resistance?
questions.explanation: R = ρL/A. When both L and A are doubled: R' = ρ(2L)/(2A) = ρL/A = R. So resistance remains unchanged.

questions.question: A 2 kW heater runs for 5 hours daily. If the cost per unit is $0.15, what is the monthly cost for 30 days?
questions.explanation: Daily energy = 2 kW × 5 h = 10 kWh = 10 units. Monthly = 10 × 30 = 300 units. Cost = 300 × $0.15 = $45.

questions.question: A 100 W light bulb is used for 10 hours. If electricity costs $0.12 per unit, what is the total cost?
questions.explanation: Energy consumed = 0.1 kW × 10 h = 1 kWh = 1 unit. Cost = 1 × $0.12 = $0.12.

questions.question: ____________ may be implied to be a material's inherent capacity or readiness to facilitate the movement of a charge.
questions.explanation: Conductivity is the measure of a material's ability to allow charge flow. It is the reciprocal of resistivity.

questions.question: What is the equivalence of one tera-ampere?
questions.explanation: The prefix 'tera-' means 10¹², so 1 tera-ampere = 10¹² amperes.

questions.question: What is the equivalence of one pico-ampere?
questions.explanation: The prefix 'pico-' means 10⁻¹², so 1 pico-ampere = 10⁻¹² amperes.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered electrical energy, power calculations, and resistivity concepts!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review cost calculations and unit conversions to strengthen your understanding.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on the resistivity formula R = ρL/A and the relationships between variables.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: learn the definitions of resistivity, conductivity, power formulas, and metric prefixes.
